title: Design a cache-aside flow
description: Implement read-through-on-miss with TTL plus write-time invalidation and stampede protection.
body: Steps: 1. On read, GET the key; on hit return it. 2. On miss, load from the source, SET with a TTL (add small random jitter to TTLs to avoid synchronized expiry), and return. 3. On write to the source, DEL the derived keys. 4. Protect hot keys from stampede with a short lock or single-flight so only one loader rebuilds while others wait or serve a slightly stale value. 5. Choose key naming so writes can compute the keys to invalidate. Rollback point: bypass the cache (always read source) to isolate cache bugs. Validation: a flushed cache still returns correct data, an update is reflected next read, and a hot-key expiry does not stampede the source.
